title: ranunculus ficaria var. calthifolia
intro: Ranunculus ficaria var. calthifolia, also known as Lesser Celandine, is a perennial plant with bright yellow flowers that grows in woodland areas and along streams. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating skin irritations and minor wounds. The health benefits of Lesser Celandine include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infections. It can be prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes.
